nativeRange.habitat: Fast-flowing rocky rapids of the lower Congo River; submerged boulders and crevices in highly oxygenated white-water zones

decorPreferences: large smooth boulders and rockwork with caves and crevices
strong water movement from multiple powerheads or wavemakers
secure cave hides (slate stacks, PVC pipes) for nocturnal retreats
minimal fine substrate — sand or bare rock acceptable
robust plants if used (Java fern, Anubias) anchored to rockwork, not substrate

diet.notes: Brichard's Syno accepts a variety of foods, including sinking pellets, carnivore tablets, frozen bloodworms, brine shrimp, and mysis shrimp. It also grazes on biofilm and algae wafers. Because the species is nocturnal, feeding after lights-out gives it the best opportunity to eat without competition. A varied diet of both meaty and plant-based foods is recommended for long-term health.

goodWith: other Lower Congo River rheophilic species
avoidWith: other Synodontis in small tanks (territorial disputes)

imageCandidates.notes: Frank M. Greco; 640x363. Only CC live photo found; sharp lateral view showing the elongated rheophile body, forked tail, barbels, and olive-yellow dark-mottled banding diagnostic of the species. Low resolution.

summary: Brichard's Syno is a rapids-specialist endemic to the lower Congo River, recognized by its bold yellow-and-dark banding and reaching up to 15 cm. Unlike most Synodontis, it requires strong turbulent flow and very high dissolved oxygen. Listed as Vulnerable by the IUCN. Suited to advanced keepers only.
careNotes: Synodontis brichardi is a rheophilic specialist endemic to the lower Congo River rapids system, one of the most hydrologically extreme freshwater habitats on Earth. Unlike the majority of Synodontis species that tolerate still or slow-moving water, S. brichardi requires strong, turbulent flow and very high dissolved oxygen levels that closely replicate its native white-water habitat. A sump or canister filter rated well above the tank volume, combined with multiple powerheads or wavemakers directed along the tank length, is essential. Oxygenation should be maximised with surface agitation; a spray bar return is beneficial. The species is cool-tolerant relative to most tropical fish, thriving at 22–25°C in line with the thermally stable Congo rapids; temperatures above 26°C should be avoided as they reduce dissolved oxygen and stress the fish. Water should be soft to moderately hard and near-neutral to slightly acidic, matching the Congo’s blackwater-influenced rapids chemistry. The tank must be furnished with large smooth boulders, stable rock stacks, and caves; the fish uses crevices as daytime retreats and becomes active after lights-out. Secure all rocks to prevent collapse injuries. Like all mochokid catfish, S. brichardi lacks true scales and is copper-sensitive — avoid all copper-based medications and treatments. Tankmates must tolerate fast current; slow-water species will be stressed and small fish risk predation at night. Keep in spacious tanks of 200 L or more with a long footprint to sustain the required flow patterns. The species is IUCN Vulnerable (2019 assessment) primarily because its very restricted range in the lower Congo hydropower development corridor places it at risk from impoundment projects.
breedingNotes: Breeding in captivity has not been reliably documented. The extreme flow and oxygenation requirements of the lower Congo rapids have proved very difficult to replicate at the scale needed to trigger spawning. No reliable accounts of aquarium reproduction exist in hobbyist or scientific literature. Captive specimens are almost exclusively wild-caught.

primary.name: SeriouslyFish — Synodontis brichardi
primary.notes: Primary source. Provided adult size (to 15 cm SL), temperature (22–25°C), pH (6.5–7.5), hardness, native range (lower Congo rapids, DRC), habitat (fast-flowing rocky rapids, high O2), diet (omnivore), care notes (strong flow essential, nocturnal, copper-sensitive, IUCN Vulnerable), and compatibility guidance.

additional.name: FishBase — Synodontis brichardi
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (15 cm TL), taxonomy (Mochokidae, Siluriformes), native range (lower Congo River, DRC only), temperature range, and diet. Confirmed endemic status.

additional.name: IUCN Red List — Synodontis brichardi
additional.notes: Confirmed IUCN Vulnerable status (2019 assessment). Threat: hydropower impoundment of lower Congo River reducing and fragmenting the species’ only known habitat. Narrow endemic; no other populations outside lower Congo rapids.

additional.name: Practical Fishkeeping — Fish of the Month: Synodontis brichardi
additional.notes: Supplementary husbandry detail: strong water movement, rocky cave decor, nocturnal feeding schedule, cool temperature preference, and yellow banding colouration confirmed. Noted rarely bred in captivity.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op — Synodontis Catfish Care
additional.notes: General Synodontis care guide consulted for genus-level diet, copper sensitivity, and nocturnal behaviour context. No dedicated S. brichardi page available; S. brichardi-specific data taken from SeriouslyFish and FishBase.

fish.breedingNotes: Captive breeding of this species has not been documented. Replicating the extreme oxygen-rich, turbulent conditions of the lower Congo rapids at an aquarium scale has so far proved impractical, and virtually all specimens in the hobby are wild-caught.
